rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HIDEKI KRIS OKA
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/27/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) As Counsel/Executor is well aware, the only correct form of notice is the mandatory Judicial Council form, Notice of Hearing, on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. If a pleading form of proof of service is filed, a copy of the JC form DE-120 that was mailed to the interested parties must be attached and must show that the instructions for how to appear remotely were attached to the notice. A proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any other papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by 4/13/26. Executor was granted additional time to file the I&A but still has not done so. If the issues with this petition are not addressed timely and the I&A is not also filed before 4/13/26, an appearance will be required on 4/27/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL FLOERSCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/23/2026. Issues necessitating continuance: 1) Detailed schedules of receipts and gains or losses on sale where an amount other than the amount of the Inventory and Appraisal is used as a basis for calculating fees on commissions (CRC 7.550(b)). The amended inventory and appraisal filed 09/10/2025 values the estate's assets at $141,075.50, but the statutory compensation calculation uses $254,000. 2) The petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed to each distributee, including the number of shares of securities and the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) to be distributed to each beneficiary (San Francisco Local Rule 14.35.I.6.a). Please address the above issues and deliver courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 03/09/2026 to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT JOHN GEARY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/27/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: An amendment was just e-filed on 1/16/26, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing and was past the deadline by when courtesy copies were to be received by the assigned examiner. No courtesy copies have been received by the assigned examiner even as of 1/23/26, so the papers have not been reviewed. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] Please provide a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of the amendment and any other papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/13/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must BEGIN with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] The rest of the order caption should disclose the relief being granted, as with a petition caption.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT J. KORMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Court to determine whether to take the petition off calendar, as the following procedural issues remain unaddressed: 1) Petition is incomplete: a) In Item 5a, box (3) or (4) must be checked. b) In Item 5a, box (7) or (8) must be checked. 2) Please clarify whether Nancy Ryti is a beneficiary of the Nancy E. Ryti Spousal Trust. 3) No proof of publication has been filed (Probate Code Sections 8120 and 8121). 4) No Notice of Petition to Administer Estate (Form DE-121) for the amended petition has been filed (Probate Code Section 8100). An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) as the pleading it amends (CRC 7.53(a)). 5) Please review San Francisco Local Rule 14.20: Petitions for probate of lost wills must clearly state on their face that the will is lost and both the published and mailed notice must so state. An additional hard copy of the lost will must be provided to the clerk for a will file to be created before the hearing to admit the lost will to probate. A copy of the written statement of the testamentary words, or their substance, or a copy of the lost will, must be attached to the proposed order. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE WHILT LIVING TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 18, 1998, TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Asset
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/25/2026. Issues necessitating a continuance: 1) Each heir of decedent must be given notice of the petition (Probate Code Sections 851 (b)(3) and 17203(c)(1)). In a verified supplement to the petition, please identify decedent's heirs and give notice of the instant petition. 2) Paragraph 17 of the instant petition requests the court's order confirm any further accounts titled in decedent's name, individually, as trust assets. Please provide authority for this request. Please address the above issues and deliver courtesy copies of all further fillings to this examiner no later than 03/11/2026 to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROWLEY AND LONNIE WONG REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Pursuant To Cal. Probate Code 850 A 3 A And 850 B; Request For Statement Of Decision; Objection To Hearsay And Out Of Court Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/25/26. Examiner's Notes emailed to attorney with the following threshold issues: 1. The petition has not been verified by the petitioner. An unverified pleading may be proved by filing a supplemental declaration that identifies the pleading by caption and its filing date [Local Rule 14.15C]. 2. Venue has not been addressed. Please be sure to discuss Probate Code 17002 and 17005 and file the Probate Code 16061.7 Notice provided by current trustee. 3. A copy of the trust has not yet been filed. 4. Notice of Hearing has not been filed. 5. Proposed order has not been filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE IRA KURLANDER 2024 REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST DATED MAY 8, 2024
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Assets To Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/4/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The exhibits were not attached to the petition, making the petition incomplete. (2) There was also no Exhibit D attached to the exhibits e-filed on 10/22/26, so the heirs and beneficiaries and their last known addresses have not been set forth in a verified pleading. (3) The proof of service indicates that notice was served without exhibits attached, making notice defective. A copy of the petition and any supplements (or amendments) thereto must be served on all interested parties, pursuant to Probate Code Section 851(a) and CRC 7.53(b), after the supplement (or amendment) is e-filed. Please provide courtesy copies to the assigned examiner of all new papers filed to address these issues by 4/20/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: Prob. C. Sect. 850 is a shortcut for a formal probate proceeding. A beneficiary of the trust receives mailed notice with a copy of the petition, but any heir-at-law, who would stand to inherit, if the trust did not exist or were invalid, is a party whose right, title or interest would be affected by this petition, and, therefore, must receive (or waive) personal service of notice. [Prob. C. Sect. 17203(b) and CCP Sect. 413.10 et seq.]
